figures.caption: Table 2. Evaluation results of baselines and TraceDev on two datasets using Gemini-2.5-Flash and DeepseekV3.2. “Cov.” denotes semantic-coverage rate and “Success” denotes success rate. “Files”, “LOC”, and “Tokens” denote the number of code files, normalized lines of code, and code token count, averaged per use case.
Fig. 3. A comparison of ETour and SMOS use cases, along with the Requirement Refiner Agent’s prompt that contains a formal syntactic constraint.

Fig. 6. A traceability graph generated by TraceDev using Gemini-2.5-Flash. Due to space limitations, only a subset of the graph is shown for clarity. The traceability graph is based on the ETOUR use case from Figure 3.

Table 3. Efficiency evaluation of TraceDev and baseline approaches.“Token Usage” denotes the average number of tokens consumed per use case, “Tim” represents the average execution time. “Files”, “LoC” and “Code Token” denote the number of code files, normalized lines of code, and code token count, respectively. “Time effect.” denotes the time effectiveness. The results for each approach are averaged across all use cases.
